text.title: Risk Management Examples
text.summary: Worked example: key-person threat scored and tracked through one weekly cycle.

example.title: Key-developer-leaves risk — full row + response plan
example.context: Mid-tier engineering programme, 4-month delivery, key developer holds 70% of payments-module commits. HR attrition base-rate for this seniority/team = 0.30.
example.code:
| ID | Description | Cat | P | I | Score | Strategy | Owner | Trigger | Source | Status | |-------|------------------------------|--------|---|---|-------|----------|-------------|---------------------------------|-------------------------|--------| | R-014 | Key developer leaves | people | M | H | 0.18 | mitigate | Tech Lead | resignation notice filed (HRIS) | hr-attrition-table.md | active |
EMV = 0.30 × $30,000 = $9,000 (rolled into contingency total).
Response plan: - Cross-train: rotate payments-module ownership over next 3 sprints. - Knowledge doc: capture undocumented design decisions in ADRs by sprint 2. - Residual EMV after mitigation: $3,000.
example.commentary: P=M with rationale (HR base-rate 0.30). I=H with rationale (cost-estimation baseline shows 4-week delay = $30k). EMV $9k pre-mitigation, $3k residual added to contingency total. Trigger is observable (HRIS notice).
